meta.title: PTE Predictive Nomogram (1)
meta.cff_info:
Post Traumatic Brain Injury Epilepsy Predictive Nomogram (1)

Wang et al., 2021

DOI: 10.1016/j.seizure.2021.03.023

Factors and Points

  • Loss of Consciousness (LOC):
    • 0 points: ≤30 min
    • 59 points: 31 min - 24 h
    • 67 points: >24 h
  • Early Post-Traumatic Seizures (EPTS):
    • 0 points: No
    • 74 points: Yes
  • Sex:
    • 0 points: Female
    • 37 points: Male
  • Treatment:
    • 0 points: Conservative
    • 43 points: Puncture
    • 51 points: Surgery
  • Subdural Hematoma (SDH):
    • 0 points: No
    • 57 points: Yes
  • Contusion Site:
    • 0 points: No
    • 29 points: Other sites
    • 65 points: Frontal temporal lobe
  • Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS):
    • 0 points: Mild
    • 76 points: Moderate
    • 100 points: Severe

Risk Estimates

Predicted risk of epilepsy at 1 and 5 years based on the score:

Score 1-Year Risk 5-Year Risk
200 10% 10%
238 10% 10%
291 20% 20%
325 30% 30%
350 40% 40%
372 50% 50%
392 60% 60%
411 70% 70%
432 80% 80%
457 90% 90%
476 95% 95%

Clinical Utility

The PTE Predictive Nomogram (1) assists in personalized risk prediction for post-traumatic brain injury epilepsy, guiding clinical decisions for anti-seizure medication and monitoring.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Score Name PTE Predictive Nomogram (1)
Population Derivation: 1301 patients (West China Hospital, 2011-2017). Validation: 834 patients (two independent cohorts).
Statistical Methods Multivariable Cox proportional hazards regression.
Validation Methods Internal: Retrospective cohort. External: Two independent cohorts (Chengdu Shang Jin Nan Fu Hospital and Sichuan Provincial People’s Hospital).
Performance Metrics C-index: 0.846 (training), 0.895 (validation).
Generalizability High (validated in two separate cohorts).
Clinical Utility Guides targeted treatment and follow-up.
meta.authors: Wang X, Zhong J, Lei T, et al.
meta.outcome: Development and external validation of a predictive nomogram model of posttraumatic epilepsy: A retrospective analysis

predictors.name: Loss of Consciousness (LOC)
predictors.assess: Loss of consciousness duration after TBI Please select the approximate length of time the patient was unconscious after the traumatic brain injury. 0–30 minutes: Brief or no observed LOC 31 minutes to 24 hours: Moderate LOC Over 24 hours: Prolonged unconsciousness Note: Longer LOC is associated with a higher risk of developing posttraumatic epilepsy (PTE).

predictors.name: Early Post-Traumatic Seizures (EPTS)
predictors.assess: Seizures occurring within the first 7 days after TBI, where their presence substantially raises the risk of developing PTE.

predictors.name: Sex
predictors.assess: Male sex is associated with a higher risk of PTE compared to females.

predictors.name: Treatment
predictors.assess: The type of treatment received after TBI, with surgical interventions linked to the highest risk of PTE.

predictors.name: Subdural Hematoma (SDH)
predictors.assess: The presence of subdural hematoma significantly elevates the risk of PTE compared to its absence.

predictors.name: Contusion Site
predictors.assess: Location of brain contusion identified on imaging with brain contusions in the frontal-temporal lobe associated with the greatest risk of PTE.

predictors.name: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS):
predictors.assess: Severity of TBI as measured by GCS at admission, with severe scores (3–8) presenting the highest risk of PTE.

model.note: Total nomogram points map to the 1-year risk of post-traumatic epilepsy (Wang 2021, Suppl. Table S5). The 5-year risk is higher for the same score (e.g. ~317 pts ≈ 30% at 1 yr, ~45% at 5 yr).

provenance.notes: Predictors/points/rows from CFF export; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
